title: Chapter 4 - Head & Meta
description: Check knowledge of the common head elements and meta tags.

explanations.overview: <title> belongs in the head and appears in the browser tab.

byOptionId.head: Correct — <title> belongs inside <head> and shows in the browser tab.
byOptionId.body: Incorrect — <title> should be placed inside <head>, not in <body>.
byOptionId.html: Incorrect — <title> must be inside <head> within the <html> element.
byOptionId.meta: Incorrect — <meta> is for metadata like charset or viewport, not the page title.

options.label: Sets character encoding so text displays correctly

explanations.overview: Character encoding ensures characters like accents show properly.

byOptionId.encoding: Correct — charset sets the document character encoding so text renders correctly.
byOptionId.style: Incorrect — styling is handled by CSS, not the charset meta tag.
byOptionId.linkcss: Incorrect — CSS is linked with <link>, not with a charset meta tag.
byOptionId.runjs: Incorrect — meta tags do not execute JavaScript.
byOptionId.language: Incorrect — the charset meta tag does not set the page language.

questions.prompt: Why add the viewport meta tag on mobile pages?

options.label: To make the page load faster
To track your data and sell it to Mark Zuckerberg
To make the page scale correctly on small screens

explanations.overview: Viewport controls layout on mobile devices so designs are responsive.

byOptionId.responsive: Correct — the viewport tag helps pages scale properly on phones and tablets.
byOptionId.speed: Incorrect — viewport affects layout, not network speed.
byOptionId.security: Incorrect — viewport does not add security features.
byOptionId.MarkZuckerberg: Incorrect — viewport doesn't control tracking or selling data.
